content: The original Reflexion agent framework can be enhanced by drawing on the language learning concept of structured guided reflection (from the high school English vocabulary unit) to refine LLM self-reflection mechanisms: just as language students repeat guided reflection to internalize correct usage and correct lexical/grammatical errors, LLMs can use spaced, scaffolded reflection prompts aligned with human language learning progression to reduce inconsistent self-evaluation and improve long-form reasoning. This turns generic self-reflection into a incremental skill-building process for LLMs, rather than a one-off correction step.
